briefing.headline: Rig up a Medium Diving Crankbait — finesse fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Medium Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland impoundments, and smallmouth lakes.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: The DT-6 is the most universal crankbait; it covers the 3-6 foot sweet spot and works from mid-40s water temps through pre-spawn, post-spawn, summer, and fall on rivers, highland and lowland
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, flat, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: Position boat outside the grass line, fire at a 45° quartering angle into shallower grass. Crank down into the grass, slow up — let the bait float free or rip it free. That stop-start-rip action
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, flat, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

KVD: In cold water, target transition areas where bass will stage before moving to spawning bays. Channel swing ends, flat edges, steeper banks transitioning to flatter areas, where different rock types come together. Chunk rock banks, bluffs, and...

Robertson: A wind direction change can destroy a deep-water pattern by muddying previously clear water. When visibility drops from 6 feet to 2 feet, bass stop moving through the area and the deep bite dies. Be ready to switch to a shallow backup p...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal fall fish pull tighter to cover and deeper structure at 8-18ft. The shad migration pauses but doesn't stop. Focus on the deepest fish in a given creek arm — channel bends and main creek points.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
